transcript.text: Hello, this video is what I think about slavery.
Slavery is bad. Slavery is very bad. I do not agree with slavery.
Obviously it should be illegal, everybody knows that, but I think slavery in general is bad.
It's always been bad. Even in the Roman times, it's bad.
Sometimes people like to justify slavery.

Like in Roman times, because of slavery, they were able to build all those monuments and rich people could have all that extra time to ponder and philosophize and write and make Rome a better civilization that way.
Or maybe, you know, because of the slaves, the pyramids were built and stuff like this.
But I don't agree with that. I think that's just a stupid excuse.
And because there was slavery in Rome, and there was slavery for such a long time, I'm sure that's why the Americans justified having slavery.
If the Romans did it and they were such a great civilization, well, we can have it too.
I think slavery should have been nipped in the bud a long time ago.

Yeah. Slavery was never, I mean, it may have been like common thing to do back in Roman times and even other cultures back in the ancient world, but it was never, I'm sure it was always argued whether it was good or not.
And there were always people who didn't like it.
I mean, with the Spartacus revolt, slave revolt in Italy, you know, there are lots of discontented slaves.
I mean, slavery and wealth disparagement are two completely different things.
And I remember, I saw a Julius Caesar movie the other day, and Julius Caesar's daughter had a slave tutor who was really smart, who was Greek, and she's like, oh, you should let Apollonius go free.
And he goes, oh, but if I did that, I wouldn't be able to afford him.
Ha ha.
But that's not true.
I mean, there would have been enough wealth disparity where, well, I mean, where you could, like Julius Caesar could have been able to afford a tutor for his daughter.
I mean, maybe not a live-in, but, I mean, slavery was the cause of, I guess you could justify it as like people would, if without slavery, then the slaves just would have quit and gone home.
But, I mean, it's definitely food for thought, but it's not, I don't agree with that.
I mean, there always would have had workers to do stuff.
People want civilization.
Part of what let Rome go on for so long was all of the pride people had in having a great culture and a great city that people, you know, they like to build stuff.
And people say, oh, but, you know, Rome was rich because they would plunder other countries and bring their people back as slaves and steal their stuff.
I don't think that's a good representation of how Romans made their money and were so powerful.
I think Rome, they did get a lot of money that way.
But one interesting thing I think to do would be like superimpose like an alternative universe where there was a Rome, but there was no slavery.
And I think it's entirely possible that you could have a Rome or, you know, an ancient civilization with no slavery and still have all the art and literature and everything.
Artists do art because they love to.
Michelangelo and Leonardo da Vinci, all they did was art.
They did art all the time because they loved it.
I mean, they had plenty of money and they had plenty of workers and stuff helping them out.
But I don't think their workers were slaves.
They just, slavery is just telling somebody what they can't do, where they can go and can't go.
And it ends up, you know, people might say, oh, but it makes it cheap to make the infrastructure.
I think it ends up making it more expensive because it makes the need for war.
Rome was always having to fight frontier battles because the people who lived on the frontier didn't want to be slaves.
If slavery didn't exist, then people would have been like, oh, cool.
Well, maybe I'll go to Rome because I want to.
I want to see that city.
I want to go and work.
And even if I don't make much money, just to be part of that.
And I don't think slavery was ever, ever justified.
And especially in America.
I don't know if slavery in America was entirely racist.
Maybe it was just they did it because, you know, it's so easy to tell a black person from a white person
That it would be easier to just document who should and shouldn't be a slave.
But maybe it was.
But I had, like, a great-great-grandfather or something who had slaves because he lived in the South.
But as soon as the war broke out, he let all his slaves go.
But that would be cool to, like, make a movie of, like, an alternative universe where there's Rome with no slaves.
I think we would have advanced as a culture a lot faster without slaves than with slaves.
There would have been a lot less wars.
And, you know, slavery produces idleness for the people who use the slaves.
And also wealth disparity, too, I think.
But that's a little bit different.
But I was, like, in Chile.
And I was a tutor for a really rich lady.
And she had, like, a part-time job.
I don't think she worked that much because her dad was really rich and stuff.
But she had, like, two, she had three kids and two live-in workers who were there, like, all the time.
Like, a 9-to-5 job helping her do stuff.
And because there's so much wealth disparity there, like, rich people would make, like, 60 times more than, like, the minimal wage.
And that's just, I mean, that's not necessarily Americans.
We get by without having two live-in workers, you know.
It's not, I think it produces more laziness than it frees up time to let people do stuff.
You can always, there's always, like, in Buddha and Buddhists in Tibet, you know, you have monasteries.
And people would give 10% of what they make to the monks, and they would go on and be scholars and keep that tradition alive and all the art and everything.
And they didn't have slaves.
So, I mean, I know it's common knowledge, pretty much, that slavery is bad, but not totally.
I mean, I think there are some people out there who would justify it.
And slavery has always been bad.
But if I, I like to believe that if I was living in those times, in Roman times, I would have been out there trying to make slavery illegal.
So, anyway, I just thought of that way today.
I figured I'd do a video.
